title: Model, Surface, Capability, and Constraint Selection · Prompt Forge
summary: Choose between Claude and Codex surfaces for a repository and document task. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a capability and surface decision matrix while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose chat, coding-agent, API, or workflow surfaces according to state, tools, latency, and review needs.
Verify current model and tool capabilities from official documentation rather than remembered names.
Match reasoning effort and autonomy to task complexity, reversibility, and verification cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-provider-portability-w75flw" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Provider Portability into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-provider-portability-w75flw
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the workshop evidence note for Provider Portability: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Use a changed workshop case for prompt model surface that tests this competency: Separate stable task contracts from provider-specific syntax and capability assumptions.. steps.why: Retrieving Provider Portability through the concrete a capability and surface decision matrix case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate stable task contracts from provider-specific syntax and capability assumptions.
